summary: Gruber provides a logistics update for his live Talk Show podcast recording at WWDC 2017 in San Jose. The first 500 tickets sold out in 7 minutes, and he announces a second batch going on sale. He explains that all tickets are now general admission after consulting with the California Theatre staff. He also outlines the streaming plan: live audio only, with edited video to follow quickly after the event.
key_points: First batch of 500 tickets sold out in 7 minutes, demonstrating the show's popularity
All tickets converted to general admission after theater staff recommended filling orchestra first, then balcony
Live video streaming is being dropped in favor of quickly-edited post-show video, with live audio still available
The event includes an open bar starting at 6pm with the show at 7pm at the California Theatre in San Jose
money_quotes: I hate writing that because it sounds braggy, but I'm putting it out there just as fair warning.
You're going to have to act quick and maybe get lucky.
This year we are not going to attempt to stream live video.
If you don't have an iPhone (?) bring a copy of the ticket PDF.
key_insight: A logistical update post that inadvertently reveals how Gruber's Talk Show had become a major cultural event within the Apple community, with demand far outstripping supply.
